title: campanula patula subsp. patula
intro: Campanula patula subsp. patula, also known as the rampion bellflower, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Campanulaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Campanula patula subsp. patula include its ability to alleviate digestive problems, reduce inflammation, and support immune function, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health conditions. Campanula patula subsp. patula can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or topical cream, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
